title: Communism As Capitalism's Weapon
subtitle: A cleaned reading of Game Theory #8
voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the lecture. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Speculative and controversial claims remain attributed to this dated source.
dek: A source-grounded reading of Jiang's lecture on the false capitalism-communism dialectic: communism appears not as capitalism's opposite but as a weapon that clears away monarchy, religion, nationalism, democracy, and social democracy so capital can reorganize the world.
thesis: The lecture turns the Cold War picture inside out. Capitalism and communism are not treated as black-and-white enemies. They share enemies: king, church, nation, democracy, and finally social democracy. In Jiang's model, communism begins as a way to make the demand for fair distribution look fanatical, conspiratorial, and impossible. Then the game gets away from its players. A propaganda weapon becomes Bolshevism; Bolshevism works because capitalist greed finances it; once 1917 succeeds, communism becomes a real world-historical script.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The puzzle is China. If capitalism and communism are true opposites, China's post-1980s movement into capitalism should have looked like a civilizational break. Instead it was fast, smooth, and successful. That is the crack in the Cold War picture. From there the lecture builds a stranger model: communism may be capitalism's weapon, or even its virus, because it destroys the older structures capitalism cannot easily defeat by itself.

marks.text: the crack in the Cold War picture
capitalism's weapon, or even its virus

beats.heading: The False Dialectic
beats.summary: China's smooth capitalist turn breaks the simple capitalism-versus-communism picture.

paragraphs.text: The ordinary story says the twentieth century was a struggle between two polar opposites: capitalism on one side, communism on the other. The lecture begins by making that picture answer for China. A communist state reforms in the 1980s and becomes deeply capitalist without the kind of violent rupture that marked feudalism giving way to capitalism or Catholic Europe giving way to another order.

marks.text: A communist state reforms in the 1980s and becomes deeply capitalist

paragraphs.text: So the dialectic is false. Communism and capitalism may be more similar than different. The sharper claim is not merely that they coexist, but that communism is a creation of capitalism, a weapon aimed at capitalism's real enemies.

marks.text: the dialectic is false
a weapon aimed at capitalism's real enemies

paragraphs.text: Those enemies are king, church, nation, and democracy. Kings can cancel debts and redistribute land. Religion teaches that money is spiritually dangerous. Nationalism blocks capital from flowing wherever return is highest. Democracy lets workers vote to redistribute wealth. Communism attacks all four.

marks.text: king, church, nation, and democracy

beats.heading: The Virus For Capital
beats.summary: Communism destroys old loyalties, then capitalism spreads into the cleared space.

paragraphs.text: The party replaces the monarch, but the party is still an oligarchy. Religion is abolished and money is what remains. National identity is subordinated to class identity. Democracy is distrusted because workers are said to have false consciousness and therefore need a vanguard to lead them. Capitalism cannot openly destroy these older loyalties. Communism can.

marks.text: Capitalism cannot openly destroy these older loyalties. Communism can.

paragraphs.text: That is why China matters twice. First it raises the puzzle; then it becomes evidence. The Cultural Revolution destroys religion, tradition, and cultural identity. Afterward, in Jiang's telling, China becomes capitalist and then hyper-capitalistic. The virus did not kill capital. It killed the inherited antibodies.

marks.text: The virus did not kill capital. It killed the inherited antibodies.

paragraphs.text: Marx's biography is then made suspicious. He writes in Britain, the most capitalist country of the nineteenth century. He lives better than an impoverished revolutionary should. Engels's money comes from an industrialist father. The question is not proof; Jiang explicitly calls the larger thesis speculative. The question is why capitalist Britain and capitalist money keep appearing around the theorist of world revolution.

marks.text: why capitalist Britain and capitalist money keep appearing

beats.heading: Socialism Is Made Unusable
beats.summary: Social democracy is the natural response to capitalism, so Marxism makes it look extreme, confiscatory, and conspiratorial.

paragraphs.text: Socialism begins as a simple majority logic. Industrial society creates workers, a middle class, and capitalists. Capital consolidates until even many capitalists lose out. Workers want relief; the middle class wants higher status; democracy lets them act together. Redistribution is the natural pressure of industrial politics.

marks.text: Redistribution is the natural pressure of industrial politics

paragraphs.text: Then Marxism makes the idea more extreme. Socialism redistributes property; communism destroys property. Socialism can unite poor and middle class; communism makes the middle class fear losing status, property, and distinction. The vanguard party promises to create true consciousness and then voluntarily disappear. Jiang's answer is blunt: power has never done that before.

marks.text: Socialism redistributes property; communism destroys property
power has never done that before

paragraphs.text: This is the game-theory move: if you want to destroy a popular idea, make it more extreme until people recoil from it. The old order needs to divide bourgeoisie, middle class, and workers after 1848. Class conflict, abolition of property, and secret international conspiracy do the dividing work.

marks.text: make it more extreme until people recoil from it

paragraphs.text: The Communist Manifesto is read through this lens. Central banking looks like the Bank of England. Communications centralization looks like an oligarchy or party. State factories and compulsory education look useful for capital as well as communism. The communist agenda is not alien enough. It keeps matching capitalist needs.

marks.text: It keeps matching capitalist needs

beats.heading: The Bolshevik Gamble
beats.summary: The Russian case becomes Jiang's main evidence that communists and capitalists worked together.

paragraphs.text: The manifesto's own language is made to sound self-indicting: the proletariat needs a party, the party takes power, and somehow the party is supposed to abolish its own supremacy. It also tells local reformers that they are part of a global conspiracy. That is politically strange if the goal is to reassure ordinary people who want a fairer society.

marks.text: the party is supposed to abolish its own supremacy

paragraphs.text: Then comes 1917. The Bolsheviks seize power but are not popular; Jiang emphasizes their minority vote share and the civil-war map around them. Whites, Greens, peasants, Orthodox loyalty, allied armies, and the Romanov mystique all make Bolshevik victory look improbable.

marks.text: Bolshevik victory look improbable

paragraphs.text: The Romanovs are the hinge. If the family lives, its wealth remains tied to the dynasty. If the family dies, Jiang says, foreign banks can keep the money. The murder is therefore recoded from fanaticism into finance. It enrages peasants, Orthodoxy, and European royalty, but it frees enormous overseas accounts from living claimants.

marks.text: recoded from fanaticism into finance

paragraphs.text: The second hinge is payment. If the Bolsheviks are unpopular and weak, how do they pay mercenaries? The answer given is Wall Street and the City of London. Red Terror becomes a debt-payment machine: loot the nobility, churches, banks, palaces, and museums; strip the society; fence value abroad; pay for the war in capitalist hard currency.

marks.text: Red Terror becomes a debt-payment machine
partners in world conquest

beats.heading: China Uses The Facade
beats.summary: A student question turns the thesis back to China, where communism becomes legitimacy, industrialization, and sovereignty.

paragraphs.text: The student asks the obvious question: if China professes communism while pursuing capitalism, is communism simply the official language for a capitalist reality? Jiang answers by separating Marx, Lenin, and Mao. Marx looked for revolution in industrial Germany, not agrarian Russia or China. Lenin says the party can accelerate history. Mao goes further: he does not even need workers. Peasants can make the revolution, and industrialization can come afterward.

marks.text: Peasants can make the revolution, and industrialization can come afterward

paragraphs.text: So Mao's revolution is not treated as a pure communist-theoretical event. It is a peasant uprising wearing communist legitimacy. The facade gets Soviet support, a framework for rapid industrialization, and a way to keep national sovereignty. Communism matters less than industrialization and freedom from Soviet control.

marks.text: a peasant uprising wearing communist legitimacy

beats.heading: The Psyop Becomes History
beats.summary: The final answer explains communism as a runaway mutation of social democracy.

paragraphs.text: The last student question asks whether communism simply equals capitalism, or whether pursuing communism finally achieves capitalism. Jiang's answer is more layered. Capitalism is the belief that society can be structured around capital. It threatens the old order of religion, king, and military. But capitalism also gives birth to social democracy, and social democracy threatens both capital and the old order.

marks.text: society can be structured around capital

paragraphs.text: That is where communism is born in the final model. Old order and capital mutate social democracy into something frightening: revolutionary, property-destroying, conspiratorial, and universal. But this is not a clean master plan. It is organic, confusing, and unstable. At first communism is a psyop; then it becomes mainstream; then Bolshevism, which once looked insane, becomes possible.

marks.text: Old order and capital mutate social democracy
At first communism is a psyop; then it becomes mainstream

paragraphs.text: The October Revolution is the wild gamble that works. Once it works, the possibility-space of the world changes. Communism becomes imaginable everywhere, including China. The lecture ends by sending students into a break to watch current events: Iran, Greenland, Canada, China. The historical model is not finished; it is being carried forward into the next world-game cycle.

marks.text: The October Revolution is the wild gamble that works

questions.question: Is Chinese-style socialism a way to profess communism while actually pursuing capitalism?
questions.answer: Jiang answers that Marx did not center agrarian China or Russia; Lenin made the party the accelerator; Mao used a communist facade over a peasant uprising because he needed legitimacy, Soviet support, industrialization, and national sovereignty.

questions.question: Does capitalism equal communism, or does pursuing communism finally achieve capitalism?
questions.answer: Jiang answers that capitalism creates social democracy, social democracy threatens both old order and capital, and communism mutates that threat into an extreme form that can delegitimize it; once Bolshevism succeeds, the mutation becomes a real historical force.

source_note: This reading preserves Jiang's January 29, 2026 speculative and controversial causal model. Claims about Wall Street, the City of London, Bolshevik finance, and Romanov assets remain attributed to this source and should not be treated as independent project conclusions.
